fields.description:
## Context

An assessment of rebar's config surface (`src/rebar/_config_schema.py` vs this
project's `rebar.toml`) identified settled rollout flags and retired alternatives to
prune, one settled feature to activate by dogfooding, and an authenticated-identity
rollout to stand up. Each decision is backed by a code-level investigation recorded on
the relevant child. This epic delivers all of it plus a doc-accuracy sweep.

## Workstreams

- **Prune (removal approved):** `reconciler.baseline_dual_write` +
  `baseline_consumer_swap` (→ always-on); the three settled verify defaults
  `progressive_drift_refresh` / `remediation_mode` / `novelty_drop_active` (→ always-on);
  `verify.require_signature_for_close` (retired alternative); and
  `compact.emit_legacy_signature_mirror` (a one-way door — landed behind a readiness-gate
  check). That is **seven** config keys grouped into four removal stories.
- **Activate by dogfooding:** `verify.completion_floor_active` — assessed as fully
  implemented AND already calibrated (gold-set 30/30, κ=1.0, zero must-never-suppress
  anchors dropped; docs/calibration/completion_floor.md). Enable it in THIS project.
- **Authenticated-identity rollout (setup only):** stand up signing identity for every
  ticket-event writer (local dev; AWS review-bot + auto-lander; GitHub Actions reconciler
  + canary), AWS/GitHub sharing the Rebar Bot identity. The `require_authenticated` flag
  flip is OUT OF SCOPE (setup only). **The signing seam** is `_seam.append_event` /
  `_apply_authorship` (`src/rebar/_commands/_seam.py`), which stamps `author_id` /
  `author_email` / `author_sig`; the reconciler's inbound writer `_write_event_file`
  (`src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/inbound_translate.py`) bypasses it today and must be
  routed through it (or stamp+sign author fields inline) — see the per-env delivery below.
- **Doc-accuracy sweep** for every removed key + overall config-doc correctness.

## Secret delivery mechanism (per environment)

SSHSIG signing needs a private-key FILE PATH (`identity.signing_key: str | None`;
`sshsig.sign(key_path=...)`). Each writer environment delivers that path as follows
(the private key is never committed; actual secret provisioning in AWS/GitHub is an
operator/escalation step, the design is fixed here):

- **Local dev / agent clone:** the developer's own OpenSSH private key under `~/.ssh/`;
  set `identity.signing_key` in local config (or export `REBAR_IDENTITY_SIGNING_KEY`) to
  that path. Per-machine, git-ignored, never committed.
- **AWS review-bot + auto-lander:** a new SSM SecureString
  `/rebar/prod/rebar-bot-signing-key` (add to `rebar_secret_params` in `infra/terraform/ssm.tf`,
  matching the `GERRIT_BOT_TOKEN` / `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` pattern, ADR-0008);
  `infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh` materializes it to a `0600` file in a dedicated path;
  the reviewbot/autolander containers set `identity.signing_key` to that path.
- **GitHub Actions reconciler + canary:** a GitHub Actions encrypted secret
  `REBAR_BOT_SIGNING_KEY`; a workflow step writes it to a `mktemp` file, `chmod 0600`, and
  exports the path as `REBAR_IDENTITY_SIGNING_KEY` (and sets the committer email to
  `joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com` so git-email resolution maps to the bot identity). The key
  is never logged or committed.

## KEEP (assessed, not touched)

Nothing on the original KEEP list survives as keep: the signature-mirror is now a
removal (readiness-gated) and the completion floor is now an activation. There are no
remaining keep-dormant decisions in this epic's scope.

## Success Criteria

- All **seven** approved removals (`baseline_dual_write`, `baseline_consumer_swap`,
  `progressive_drift_refresh`, `remediation_mode`, `novelty_drop_active`,
  `require_signature_for_close`, `emit_legacy_signature_mirror`) are gone from the schema,
  their behavior hardcoded always-on (no dead back-out branch remains), and no
  doc/help/release-note references a removed key.
- `completion_floor_active` is enabled in this project's `rebar.toml`, with a documented
  Monitoring note naming the sidecar `dropped[]` / `drop_reason="completion"` audit
  surface.
- Every ticket-event-writing environment can sign as its intended identity (bot for
  AWS/GitHub) via the per-environment key delivery above; the reconciler code path is
  fixed to route through the signing seam; the test suite has a defensive guard so a stray
  global enforcement env can't red the build.
- `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass on the landed result.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All child stories are closed.
- [ ] `rebar` runs clean against this project's `rebar.toml` (a grep of `rebar.toml` for each removed key name returns zero hits; no `unknown key … ignored` warnings).
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass on the landed result.
- [ ] A repo-wide grep confirms no committed doc/help/release-note references any removed config key.
- [ ] Each ticket-event-writing environment (local, AWS auto-lander/review-bot, GHA reconciler + canary) has a named, documented signing-key delivery path per the "Secret delivery mechanism" section; actual secret provisioning that requires AWS/GitHub access is recorded as an operator step where it cannot be completed in-repo.
